title: AI token costs cross labor parity as Waymo laps Tesla 577 to 42 - TCR 05/30/26
description:
Glean and Factory AI CEOs told CNBC that Fortune 500 AI budgets exhaust in two months as per-token cost crosses human labor parity, Waymo unveiled the purpose-built Ojai robotaxi as Texas's new AV registry recorded 577 Waymo vehicles against just 42 unsupervised Teslas, and Cathy Tie announced a US venture to genetically modify human embryos seven years after her ex-husband He Jiankui was jailed for the first CRISPR babies.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-may-30-2026/

0:00 Kevin O'Leary and the 9-gigawatt Utah pushback
1:59 What shipped
5:36 Texas AV registry and Waymo Ojai launch
15:03 Kevin O'Leary, the 9-Gigawatt Project, and the Bots That Aren't There
19:27 Enterprise AI token cost crosses human labor parity

This episode covers the enterprise AI cost-versus-labor inflection with Wix's 20% and SentinelOne's 8% layoffs, the Texas autonomous-vehicle registry exposing the camera-only versus sensor-fusion architectural bet and Waymo's Zeekr-built Ojai launch, Shift's "magic hat" startup paying New Yorkers in free cleaning for kitchen footage, Cathy Tie's US embryo-editing announcement, and Kevin O'Leary's CCP-bot accusation against Utah opponents of his 9-gigawatt Stratos data center.

The substitution of cognitive labor by inference is compounding faster than the institutions surrounding it were built to receive. Glean's Arvind Jain told CNBC this is the first time in modern history technology costs the same as people, putting the tokens-or-humans trade-off into CFO conversations openly. Factory AI's Matan Grinberg described buyers reasserting agency by routing 95% of frontier-model usage down to cheaper tiers for ten-fold savings. The Algorithmic Bridge's 11 charts placed the structural picture side by side: ten companies carrying the broader economy, junior developer hiring collapsing, the financing math not adding up. Apollo and Blackstone are shopping a $36 billion debt deal to buy Google TPUs Anthropic will lease. Texas's autonomous-vehicle registry made the deployment gap legible for the first time, while Waymo's Ojai minivan co-developed with Geely's Zeekr launches public rides in three US cities. Shift's free-cleaning hook prices the consent layer for embodied AI training data through a checkbox in private homes. Cathy Tie's announcement ends the deferral the 2019 germline-editing moratorium was supposed to hold. Merck's calderasib won FDA Breakthrough Therapy designation for first-line KRAS G12C lung cancer and Afrezza inhaled insulin was approved for pediatric use.

Additional Reading:
- CNBC: Tokens or Humans? The New Corporate Trade-Off: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/05/29/-tokens-or-humans-the-new-corporate-trade-off.html

- Ars Technica: Startup Offers Free Home Cleaning If It Can Record It All: https://arstechnica.com/ai/2026/05/robot-training-startup-will-send-humans-wearing-cameras-to-clean-your-home/
- The Guardian: Biotech Barbie Cathy Tie on Her Mission to Genetically Modify Babies: https://www.theguardian.com/science/2026/may/30/there-is-no-way-to-stop-this-biotech-barbie-cathy-tie-on-her-mission-to-genetically-modify-babies
